content:
Hi guys, I am currently planning to rewrite tty0tty module in rust, but I noticed that support for tty module does not exist in rust for linux. In the original c module it has the following header:
#include <linux/tty.h>
#include <linux/tty_driver.h>
#include <linux/tty_flip.h>
But these headers aren't available in rust. I searched for bindgen to generate Rust-usable headers, but that tutorial doesn't seem to have been made for rust for linux development, Does anyone know how to use bindgen in rust for linux?
You can add the includes to rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h
https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust-next/rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h

Alice Ryhl said:

You can add the includes to rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h
Thanks! Now that I've added these headers to bindings_helper.h, what do I do next? Are there concrete examples or complete tutorials?
Once you add them there, the relevant symbols automatically become available from the bindings:: crate.

image.png
image.png
In my for bingdings_helper. h add after missing three modules to recompile, appeared the following information in the Linux/rust/bindings/bindings_generated. Rs. But when I try to use kernel::bindings, it gives an error. How to solve this problem? Thanks！

It looks like you are assuming that everything from linux/tty.h would end up in a submodule tty of the bindings crate, but that's not the case.
Everything from linux/tty.h will be in bindings::. For instance the tty_struct should be accessible like this: bindings::tty_struct.
Thanks for your reply! May i ask is there any real world instance of how to use bindgen to write a rust module? If anyone know about it please tell me how to do it.

Hi guys, I am currently planning to rewrite tty0tty module in rust, but I noticed that support for tty module does not exist in rust for linux. In the original c module it has the following header:
#include <linux/tty.h>
#include <linux/tty_driver.h>
#include <linux/tty_flip.h>
But these headers aren't available in rust. I searched for bindgen to generate Rust-usable headers, but that tutorial doesn't seem to have been made for rust for linux development, Does anyone know how to use bindgen in rust for linux? If any know real world example on how to use bindgen in rust for linux. Please let me know, thanks!
hi wenzhao, i would like to help. which module is tty0tty?

I found this task in a GitHub issue. It’s about rewriting the tty0tty module using Rust (https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/62). For more details about the module, check out this link: (https://github.com/freemed/tty0tty).
ahh
i understand the problem now thank you
i had assumed you were talking about a module that was inside of linux repo
it is 2238 here. i'll come back to this tomorrow

image.png
now i follow the instruction, i want to declear a struct and using other struct decleared in the bindings_generated.rs. I use bindings::tty_struct but is still report error. How can i use those struct and function?
wenzhao has marked this topic as resolved.
wenzhao has marked this topic as unresolved.

ok! Thanks for your help. By the way, other people in the zulip tell me i can use bindings:: to access the decleared struct and function in c header. But i still confused on how to use it. The link is: https://rust-for-linux.zulipchat.com/#narrow/stream/288089-General/topic/how.20to.20use.20bindgen.20in.20rust.20for.20linux.EF.BC.9F
Hope is useful for you to solve my problem. Thanks!

image.png
now my problem is just like this.
the bindings crate is not available to drivers/samples, since we require safe abstractions to interact with them. the kernel crate is the place where you can access them.

Benno Lossin said:

the bindings crate is not available to drivers/samples, since we require safe abstractions to interact with them. the kernel crate is the place where you can access them.

Thanks for your response. If I'm not considering safe abstractions for these functions right now and just want to get a simple TTY module running in the kernel using something like "unsafe extern 'C'" first, and then gradually add safety abstractions to each part, is this approach feasible?

Does anyone know how to use bindgen in rust for linux? I currently want to implement a tty0tty kernel driver module, but because of the lack of headers, I need to use bindgen. A few people told me to add headers to rust/bindings/bindings_helper.h. After I joined in, I still don't know how to use those structs in my own module. Can anyone teach me?

yes, but you only will be able to upstream it if you are using abdtractions. I dont know if/how you can make the build system give your module access to the bindings, but you can first try putting your code into the kernel crate

is this approach feasible?

this is the approach i am taking. i'm confident in it but have not yet reached a stage where i can add abstractions.

but you can first try putting your code into the kernel crate

i suggest this too
you might like to bring the c module into the kernel directory if it's not too annoying to do so. this might make it easier to work on.

that's great, could you tell me how to do it? Does i just need put the oroginal c header file in the kernel folder then i can use it, or there os other work to do? could you please give an example on how to do it. thanks!

Tommy Gilligan said:

is this approach feasible?

this is the approach i am taking. i'm confident in it but have not yet reached a stage where i can add abstractions.
i can try to work out how to do that but that'll have to be tomorrow (again, sorry). i encourage you to try in the mean-time because it sounds like you have the right idea.

You will need to wrap these binding in a Rust library (currently there's only the kernel crate), and your driver can call the pub function from the library.

You may want to check the rust/kernel/net.rs and rust/kernel/net/, but that's the only the temporary location, subsystem libraries may get moved into their own library later.
20 messages were moved here from #General > how to use bindgen in rust for linux？ by Boqun Feng.

Notification Bot said:

20 messages were moved here from #General > how to use bindgen in rust for linux？ by Boqun Feng.

Merge two topics into one.
Ok, thanks for your reply. I will wait Tommy to show an example :) thanks for all your help.

Boqun Feng said:

You will need to wrap these binding in a Rust library (currently there's only the kernel crate), and your driver can call the pub function from the library.

You may want to check the rust/kernel/net.rs and rust/kernel/net/, but that's the only the temporary location, subsystem libraries may get moved into their own library later.

I understand that if I eventually want to upstream, I need to ensure it's securely wrapped. However, right now I just want to get the tty module running, even if it's using something like unsafe extern "C". It's quite frustrating not being able to get it running at all. Does anyone know how to do this? A sample or a tutorial would be really helpful.

Tommy Gilligan said:

i can try to work out how to do that but that'll have to be tomorrow (again, sorry). i encourage you to try in the mean-time because it sounds like you have the right idea.

Tommy, could you tell me how you do it? Maybe just upload a sample to github or a screen shoot of how to do it would be helpful. Thank you!

currently building a fresh copy of 6.10-rc3
i've downloaded https://github.com/freemed/tty0tty
i'll let you know how i go
Thank you so much for your help, tommy. I'll also try to figure out how to directly call in an unsafe way and further secure encapsulation.
